title: [WIP] Vendor a branch of c/common to test Pasta as default

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [cevich](https://github.com/cevich) for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Pretty sure you need to update the VMs here as well, pasta is likely to old here and still hangs:
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/8436ffdea5e587ad7633a3a9448b247c15b1af6c/.cirrus.yml#L35
I think they're the same? Podman is using f39f38d13 as well

Check the date, 2024-02-01 so it was created before the pasta fixes.
f39f38d13 is just for the distro versions used.
This will blow up with the crun debian issue.
Yes we can ignore the crun failures. I mostly want to look at the logs and see if we have any pasta failures.

well so much for the nice and easy one
```
 pasta failed with exit code 1:
[+0926s] # No routable interface for IPv6: IPv6 is disabled
[+0926s] # Couldn't open network namespace /proc/65626/ns/net: Permission denied
[+0926s] # [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```

I think this a selinux problem, easy to reproduce locally `buildah build --network pasta .` if you have Containerfile with a RUN instruction.

 I know it also fails in podman unless we do `chcon -t container_runtime_exec_t` on it? It seems buildah is labeled `system_u:object_r:bin_t:s0`, any reason why it is not  `container_runtime_exec_t` like podman?
If I change buildah to container_runtime_exec_t it works locally.
@rhatdan Do you know?
I think this is vendored, so closing.
